questions.question: What's the actual impact of fees and slippage on retail-size paper-traded crypto strategies?
questions.summary: ~$1/round-trip on $200 positions (0.25% taker both legs). Gross +$1,085 became net -$782 on the 3-month backtest — the delta is the answer. Fees erase thin edges.

questions.question: Which parameter ranges of Bollinger and MA-crossover are robust to regime shifts, and which are overfit?
questions.summary: None are robust: all 12 variants with n>100 negative over 6 months net of costs. Per-regime conditioning untested (rung 3).

questions.question: (Emergent) Do constraints or strategies determine outcomes at this scale?
questions.summary: Constraints dominate: 99.6% of candidate signals died on cooldown/exposure/concurrency. Candidate fitness = edge per constraint slot, not signal frequency.

questions.question: Does sentiment data (Fear & Greed) improve technical strategies, or is it noise?
questions.summary: Layer 2 (context) never went live; deferred past Phase 1b per one-factor-at-a-time.

questions.question: Does the walk-forward tuner produce variants that hold up out-of-sample?
questions.summary: tune.py never built; compare.py (the scoring instrument) now exists. Blocked on the candidate roster accumulating live trades.

learnings.claim: GitHub Actions free-tier */5 cron on small/inactive public repos delivers only ~3-5% of scheduled invocations (~24/day vs assumed 288/day), not the documented cadence.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-18 (GH cron cadence recalibration) and 2026-05-23 (69 of 1,712 expected = 4.0% invocation rate)

learnings.claim: Migrating the cron to a Hetzner CX22 VPS delivers near-100% invocation reliability, satisfying the 95% uptime gate.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 (47-day gap entry: 11,490+ fetch commits every 5 min, zero gaps >1h, runs 84->11,574 all status='ok'; 95% uptime gate mechanically satisfied)

learnings.claim: Both base strategies (Bollinger mean-reversion and MA-crossover) lose money net of fees over a real 6-month window; every variant with n>100 is negative.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 (Retire Bollinger and MA-crossover: 1,371 trades, net -$1,719.44 (-0.13%), bollinger_default n=539 -$636.75, only positives at n=3 and n=13)

learnings.claim: Realistic constraints (cooldown/exposure/concurrency) dominate strategy behavior, rejecting 99.6% of candidate signals.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 (Retire entry and Front-loaded 12 variants entry: 390,519 candidates, 99.6% rejected)

learnings.claim: The shared position ceiling means adding variants does not add exposure, so all variants can run in parallel with no risk cost.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 (Front-loaded all 12 variants: total exposure never breached; P&L moved only via selection within the cap)

learnings.claim: The fee-floor failure shape (gross-positive, fee-negative) replicates on novel LLM-surfaced strategies, not just textbook ones.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 (Gauntlet results: weekend_illiquidity_momentum win rate 49.4%, gross Sharpe 1.51, but -$0.21/trade net; edge smaller than ~0.6% round-trip cost)

learnings.claim: None of the five LLM-surfaced gauntlet candidates earned promotion; all five lost money net of fees over the 180-day replay.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 (Gauntlet results: all five negative net of fees, ranked -$0.21 to -$2.56/trade)

learnings.claim: No real credential was ever committed to the repo; the .env exposure surface was the Claude Code session transcript, not the repository.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 (Post-incident record: full git-history search confirms only placeholders in .env.template, .env never tracked)

learnings.claim: Deferring incident logging destroys the facts a real postmortem needs; a ~10-minute write-up deferred ~60 days lost exact date, echoing command, and transcript persistence.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 (Post-incident record: written ~60 days late from memory, details gone)

learnings.claim: The operator stopped contributing for 47 days while the VPS infrastructure ran clean, confirming the 2026-05-23 bear case that 'the infrastructure was the project.'
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 (47-day operator gap: no human commits 2026-05-23 to 2026-07-02; signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls all still 0)

learnings.claim: The initial diagnosis that the VPS had died silently was wrong; checking the live GitHub repo directly showed the VPS never stopped.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 (47-day operator gap: local trader.db was 47 days stale, live repo showed continuous operation)

learnings.claim: Ten weeks of operation produced zero signals, decisions, and trades — the terminal gap named by both machine reviews ('2016 successful runs, 0 decisions').
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-16 (Null variant live entry) and 2026-07-02 (47-day gap: signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls all 0)

hypotheses.commitment: By end of the first calendar week of cron operation, the runs table should contain ~2,016 rows with status='ok' (90%+ success rate); if below 90%, investigate before Week 2 strategy-roster review begins.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-17 — Curriculum anchor is first cron run; cron wire-up; SQLite-in-repo as data store
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved against by the 2026-05-18 and 2026-05-23 entries: GitHub Actions delivered only ~4% invocation rate (69 of 1,712), far below 90%. The 2026-05-23 migration amended/replaced this hypothesis.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the operator commits a decision-log entry choosing one of (a) keep GH cron for fetch+signals, move execute to real scheduler; (b) move signals+execute together now; or (c) move everything to one real scheduler before Week 3. If no decision is committed by end of Week 2, the scheduler issue becomes the most expensive un-made call and Week 3 build doesn't begin.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-18 — GH cron cadence does not match assumed 5-min
hypotheses.resolution: Effectively resolved by the 2026-05-23 migration entry, which moved the cron (fetch.py) to a Hetzner VPS — a scheduler-migration decision committed in a decision-log entry.

hypotheses.commitment: 90%+ success rate by end of first calendar week of GitHub-Actions-invoked runs.
hypotheses.resolution: Amended in the 2026-05-23 entry into two separately-tracked metrics (invocation rate VPS-controlled ≥99%, code success rate ≥95%) because the platform-induced ceiling made the original unfalsifiable.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the Claude-mediated synthesis will produce either (a) a defensible justification for keeping Bollinger and MA-crossover naming which modern-filter criteria they pass/fail, or (b) a defensible alternative roster with the same justification structure. If neither, the LLM-as-epistemological-backbone thesis is weaker than expected and Week 3 starts a postmortem on the reframe itself.
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved for: the 2026-07-02 retirement entry, resolving on 6-month evidence, retired both base strategies and deferred replacement to LLM-surfaced candidates — the alternative-roster path (b).

hypotheses.commitment: If neither base strategy clears the bar over a real window, replace them.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-04-26 reframe entry (falsifiable hypothesis restated in 2026-07-02 retirement entry)
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved for on the 6-month replay evidence: both Bollinger and MA-crossover retired 2026-07-02; every n>100 variant negative.
